content.explanation:
**Advance Level Sentences Part 1 — Complex & Compound Structures:**

अब हम ऐसे sentences सीखेंगे जो educated, professional और fluent speakers use करते हैं।

**1. Conditional Sentences (If Clauses):**
• Zero Conditional (fact): If you heat water, it boils.
• First Conditional (real future): If I study, I will pass.
• Second Conditional (unreal present): If I had money, I would travel.
• Third Conditional (unreal past): If I had studied, I would have passed.

**2. Reported Speech (Indirect Speech):**
Direct: She said, "I am happy."
Indirect: She said that she **was** happy.

**3. Relative Clauses:**
The man **who called** is my boss.
The book **that I read** was amazing.
The city **where I live** is beautiful.

**4. Emphasis Structures:**
**It is** English **that** I am learning.
**What** I need is your support.
rules: Zero Conditional: If + Simple Present → Simple Present (universal truth)
First Conditional: If + Simple Present → will + V1 (real possibility)
Second Conditional: If + Simple Past → would + V1 (unreal present)
Third Conditional: If + Past Perfect → would have + V3 (unreal past)
Reported speech: tense shifts back one step (is→was, will→would, can→could)
Relative pronouns: who (person), which (thing), where (place), when (time)
Cleft sentences for emphasis: It is... that/who... / What I... is/was...
content.memoryTrick:
**CONDITIONAL STAIRS:**
0 (Fact) → Present + Present
1 (Real) → Present + Will
2 (Unreal Present) → Past + Would
3 (Unreal Past) → Had done + Would have done
Go up one step each time!

examples.hindi: अगर तुम मेहनत करो, तो सफल होगे।
examples.english: If you work hard, you will succeed.

examples.hindi: अगर मेरे पास wings होती, तो मैं उड़ता।
examples.english: If I had wings, I would fly.

examples.hindi: उसने कहा कि वह थकी है।
examples.english: She said that she was tired.

examples.hindi: जो आदमी वहाँ खड़ा है, वह मेरा boss है।
examples.english: The man who is standing there is my boss.

examples.hindi: मुझे जो चाहिए वो है तुम्हारा सहयोग।
examples.english: What I need is your support.

examples.hindi: अगर मैंने पहले शुरू किया होता, तो finish हो जाता।
examples.english: If I had started earlier, I would have finished.

mistakes.wrong: If I will study, I will pass.
mistakes.correct: If I study, I will pass.
mistakes.why: First conditional: If + Present (not 'will') → will + V1

mistakes.wrong: If I would have money, I would travel.
mistakes.correct: If I had money, I would travel.

mistakes.wrong: The book which I read it was nice.
mistakes.correct: The book which I read was nice.
mistakes.why: Relative clause replaces the pronoun — no double subject

speakingTips: Conditionals in daily talk: 'If you tell me earlier, I can arrange something.'
Relative clauses: 'The colleague who helped me yesterday is very talented.'
Interviews: 'The project that I led resulted in a 40% improvement in efficiency.'

vocabulary.example: He trained hard; consequently, he won.
Regardless of the outcome, we tried.
She is undoubtedly the best candidate.

practice.hindi: अगर मैं तुम होता, तो यह गलती कभी नहीं करता।
practice.english: If I were you, I would never make this mistake.
alternatives: were i in your place, i would not do this.
practice.hint: If I were you + would + base verb (Second Conditional)

practice.hindi: काश मेरे पास और समय होता!
practice.english: I wish I had more time!
alternatives: i wish there was more time.
practice.hint: I wish + had + noun (wish for impossible/unlikely)

practice.hindi: मुझे खेद है कि मैं वहाँ नहीं आ सका।
practice.english: I regret that I could not come there.
alternatives: i am sorry i could not make it.
practice.hint: I regret that + past tense

practice.hindi: वह इतना थका था कि खड़ा भी नहीं हो सकता था।
practice.english: He was so tired that he could not even stand.
alternatives: he was too tired to even stand up.
practice.hint: so + adjective + that + could not

practice.hindi: जितनी मेहनत करोगे, उतनी सफलता मिलेगी।
practice.english: The harder you work, the more success you will achieve.
alternatives: the more effort you put in, the more you will succeed.
practice.hint: The more/harder + you verb, the more + result

practice.hindi: न केवल वह होशियार है बल्कि वह बहुत मेहनती भी है।
practice.english: Not only is he intelligent but he is also very hardworking.
alternatives: he is not only smart but also very diligent.
practice.hint: Not only is + subject + but also

practice.hindi: मैं चाहे कितनी भी कोशिश करूँ, वह नहीं मानेगा।
practice.english: No matter how hard I try, he will not agree.
alternatives: however hard i try, he will not agree.
practice.hint: No matter how + adjective + I verb + result

practice.hindi: वह जैसे ही घर पहुँचा, बारिश शुरू हो गई।
practice.english: As soon as he reached home, it started raining.
alternatives: the moment he got home, rain began.
practice.hint: As soon as + Simple Past + Simple Past

practice.hindi: तुम्हारे बिना यह काम करना असंभव था।
practice.english: Without you, this work was impossible to do.
alternatives: it was impossible to complete this work without you.
practice.hint: Without + pronoun + was + impossible + to

practice.hindi: मैं उसके बारे में जो सुनता था, वह सच नहीं था।
practice.english: What I used to hear about him was not true.
alternatives: the things i heard about him were untrue.
practice.hint: What I used to hear + was not + adjective

practice.hindi: भले ही वह अमीर हो, वह ख़ुश नहीं है।
practice.english: Even though he is rich, he is not happy.
alternatives: despite being wealthy, he is unhappy.
practice.hint: Even though + Present + negative result

practice.hindi: उसने इतनी जल्दी में काम किया कि उसमें गलतियाँ हो गईं।
practice.english: He worked in such a hurry that mistakes crept in.
alternatives: he was in such a rush that errors appeared.
practice.hint: such a + noun + that + result

practice.hindi: तुम वही बन जाते हो जो तुम सोचते हो।
practice.english: You become what you think.
alternatives: you are what you think.
practice.hint: You become + what + you think

practice.hindi: जब तक तुम गलती मानना नहीं सीखोगे, तुम आगे नहीं बढ़ सकते।
practice.english: Until you learn to accept your mistakes, you cannot move forward.
alternatives: you cannot progress until you accept your faults.
practice.hint: Until you + learn to + accept + result

practice.hindi: जो बोया, वही काटोगे।
practice.english: You will reap what you sow.
alternatives: as you sow, so shall you reap.
practice.hint: Proverb structure: you will reap what you sow

practice.hindi: मुझे अफसोस है कि मैंने उसकी बात नहीं सुनी।
practice.english: I regret not listening to him.
alternatives: i am sorry i did not listen to him.
practice.hint: I regret + not + verb+ing

practice.hindi: वह बात करने के बजाय काम करता है।
practice.english: Instead of talking, he works.
alternatives: he works rather than talking.
practice.hint: Instead of + verb+ing + main clause

practice.hindi: उसने बिना सोचे-समझे निर्णय लिया।
practice.english: He made the decision without thinking.
alternatives: he decided without any thought.
practice.hint: made decision + without + verb+ing

practice.hindi: जितना अधिक तुम पढ़ोगे, उतना ज़्यादा जानोगे।
practice.english: The more you read, the more you will know.
alternatives: reading more leads to knowing more.
practice.hint: The more + you verb + the more + result

practice.hindi: मैं तब तक नहीं रुकूँगा जब तक मुझे जवाब नहीं मिलता।
practice.english: I will not stop until I get an answer.
alternatives: i shall not rest until i receive a reply.
practice.hint: will not stop + until + I get

mockTest.hindi: अगर मैं तुम होता, तो यह गलती कभी नहीं करता।
